summary: BAR-50 cross-repo DB packet for the superseding v2 intake: register sensor-only Talent Flow signal categories in lcs.signal_registry and apply any required lcs.signal_queue contract or constraint updates so barton-outreach-core can emit movement-detection signals into CL without inline cross-repo migration scope.
routing_notes: Aligned to superseding planner intake bar50-skeleton-v2-0a3041d0-9064-445a-bf8e-04ff0b69723b.json. Cross-repo note explicitly identified CL database work for signal category registration and queue-side compatibility. Planner resolved the PRD conflict in favor of Option B sensor-only scope on the CL side and kept this as a separate company-lifecycle-cl packet to avoid mixing CL migrations into the barton-outreach-core skeleton packet.

payload.planner_resolution: Option B sensor-only. CL scope is limited to signal category registration and queue compatibility for movement-detection emission. No cascade enrichment, sovereign_id minting, or UT orchestration logic belongs in this packet.
deliverables: Register four new sensor-only Talent Flow signal categories in lcs.signal_registry
Add TALENT_FLOW to lcs.signal_queue source_hub validation if not already allowed
Update lcs.signal_queue constraints/contracts only if required for Talent Flow emission compatibility
Produce DB changeset artifacts and verification evidence in changesets/outbox
constraints: CL packet covers only CL-side database work; no barton-outreach-core application code changes here
Preserve by-value signal registry references in lcs.signal_queue
Do not implement cascade enrichment or sovereign_id minting in CL
Follow PRD sensor-only interpretation for this cross-repo DB scope
